title: My Land.
content:
I.

    She is a rich and rare land;
    Oh! she's a fresh and fair land;
    She is a dear and rare land--
    This native land of mine.


II.

    No men than her's are braver--
    Her women's hearts ne'er waver;
    I'd freely die to save her,
    And think my lot divine.


III.

    She's not a dull or cold land;
    No! she's a warm and bold land;
    Oh! she's a true and old land--
    This native land of mine.


IV.

    Could beauty ever guard her,
    And virtue still reward her,
    No foe would cross her border--
    No friend within it pine!


V.

    Oh! she's a fresh and fair land;
    Oh! she's a true and rare land;
    Yes! she's a rare and fair land--
    This native land of mine.
meaning:
Thomas Osborne Davis's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "My Land."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
